title: Tabota Score Format v2
description: A universal description language for time-based events. v2 collapses the v1.5 hierarchy (Section / Track / ChildSection / Event) into a SINGLE recursive type: the Event. Everything in a score is an Event. An Event is a bag of optional, stackable facets — frame (the old 'regime' role), value, position, extent, containment, reference, payload. None are required: an empty Event {} is valid. This is a language of description, not a scheduler: it may express relations that are under-determined, over-determined, indeterminate, or paradoxical (cyclic). Realizers (e.g. Tabota Roll, MIDI export) attempt to find a consistent assignment of times and pitches; where none exists, the realizer reports it. The schema permits what the realizer may refuse.

profile.$comment: Advisory conformance claim. 'determinate' asserts the score uses only the realizable fragment (no cycles, no ranges left open, every position resolvable) and is expected to round-trip to MIDI losslessly. 'full' makes no such promise. The schema does not enforce this; it is a contract for tools.

score.description: The top-level Events. Equivalent to the children of an implicit root Event.

Event.description: The single universal type. Every node in a Tabota score is an Event. All facets are optional and stackable. {} is a valid Event (an undefined event somewhere in its container — Degenerate Case 1). Add 'frame' to make it a regime; add 'value' to make it a note/lane/instruction; add 'events' to make it a container; add several to make it all at once.

id.description: Optional. Only Events the composer wishes to reference (in a relation, landmark, or ref) need an id. The decision to name an Event IS the decision to make it relatable.

role.$comment: Purely advisory hint for humans and editors. Does not change interpretation — interpretation follows the facets actually present. 'regime' is just an Event that carries a frame; the word survives the type merger as a role, not a type.

frame.description: REGIME FACET. Establishes the coordinate system that this Event's CHILDREN are interpreted in, and supplies the conversion keys (bpm for time, tuning for pitch). This is a SCOPE relationship only — it does not by itself set this Event's own temporal extent (see 'extent'). Children with no frame of their own inherit the nearest ancestor frame (lexical scoping over the tree).

value.description: VALUE FACET. What this Event IS along one or more axes — a pitch, a lane, an instruction, a velocity. Stackable across axes (a pitch AND text at once). Any sub-value may be exact, gliding, a range, or relative to another Event.

position.description: POSITION FACET. When/where this Event sits, in its frame's units and/or relative to other Events. Omit entirely to let the Event float freely within its container with no defined position.

extent.description: EXTENT FACET. This Event's OWN duration / end condition — independent of which children it contains. An Event with children but no extent goes on until an end condition or until realization bounds it.

events.description: CONTAINMENT. Child Events. Any Event may contain Events; this is what makes the type recursive. A child may legally extend in time beyond this Event's own extent (containment is scope, not a temporal superset).

ref.description: REFERENCE FACET. Makes this Event an instance of another Event (local id or external file), optionally transformed. Subsumes v1.5 'reference' and 'external' event types.

payload.description: Arbitrary attached data, text, comments, performance conditions. The open extension point — additionalProperties allowed here, unlike on the Event itself.

Frame.description: The regime facet. Both halves are optional and independently inheritable — a Frame may set only time (inheriting axis from an ancestor) or only axis.

temporalSettings.$comment: anyOf, not oneOf: the sibling 'temporal' enum is the real discriminator; settings shapes overlap (an achronous frame may be near-empty), so requiring exactly one match is wrong.

Value.description: Stackable typed values. Present whichever axes apply to this Event.

lane.description: Categorical value (a named lane). Exact, or an indeterminate choice among lanes.

PitchValue.description: A pitch that is exact, gliding, an indeterminate band, or relative to another Event's pitch. Use 'hz' for frequency axis, 'note' for symbolic axis (realized to Hz via the nearest frame's tuning).

between.description: Indeterminate pitch band. Bounds in Hz or note names.

intent.$comment: 'free' = genuinely indeterminate, a realizer should preserve the band or choose within it but not pretend it was exact. 'unspecified' = the composer simply did not pin it down; a realizer may pick anything in range.

relativeTo.description: id of another Event whose pitch this one is defined against.

relation.$comment: Pitch counterpart of the temporal relations in Position. Use with relativeTo.

Scalar.description: A numeric quantity that may be exact, a range, or relative. Reused for velocity and similar.

Position.description: Any combination may be present; together they form constraints on this Event's onset. Over-determination and contradiction are permitted (the realizer resolves or reports). With nothing present, the Event floats freely in its container.

at.description: Absolute onset in the governing frame's units (beats / seconds / index).

index.description: Ordinal position in an achronous frame.

anchor.description: Pin a known internal point of this Event to a time. position 0 = start, 0.5 = middle, 1 = end.

relations.description: Relational constraints to landmarks on OTHER Events (Allen-style). An Event may carry several; together with the cross-Event graph they may form cycles, which the language permits.

between.description: Indeterminate onset window. Bounds may be numeric (frame units) or Landmarks ('sometime between Event B and Event C').

Relation.description: A qualitative temporal relation to a landmark, after Allen's interval algebra, with an optional quantitative offset.

offset.description: Quantitative gap in frame units (e.g. 'after, offset 2' = 2 beats/seconds after the landmark).

Landmark.description: A point located by reference to another Event. Since only named Events are referenceable, 'the end of Note Z in Regime A' is simply { event: 'Z', point: 'end' } — Z carries its own id.

point.$comment: 'onset' is a synonym for 'start'. Default 'end' matches the intuition that 'after A' usually means after A finishes.

Extent.description: This Event's own duration / end condition. Decoupled from containment: an Event may have a short extent yet contain children that outlast it, or have children and no extent at all.

duration.description: Length in the governing frame's units.

endAt.description: Absolute end time in frame units.

until.description: Ends at a landmark on another Event.

end.$comment: 'open' = goes on forever (a drone, an unbounded regime); representable but flagged non-exportable by realizers. 'untilChildrenEnd' = extent equals the bounding span of contained children. 'untilParentEnds' = ends when the containing Event ends.

between.description: Indeterminate duration window in frame units.

Ref.description: Instance another Event by local id or external file, optionally transformed.

target.description: Local id of the Event to instance.

externalFile.description: Direct path to an external Tabota file.

transpose.description: Pitch shift (semitones, or cents if 'unit' says so).

AchronousSettings.description: Sequential ordering without time. 'indices' is optional now: an achronous frame may simply collect its children and let them order themselves via Position.relations.
